perturbation.original_text: 8. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information except copies required for backup, disaster recovery, or business continuity and in such case the obligations hereunder shall survive until such copies are destroyed .
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient is not required to return any confidential information. All copies of the Confidential Information will be retained by the Recipient for auditing purposes .
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that upon request, the Recipient must return Confidential Information within 10 days, except for backup copies which must eventually be destroyed. The modified text completely negates this obligation, stating the Recipient is not required to return any information, they will retain for auditing purposes. The modified clause creates a direct contradiction by reversing the return obligation, thus contradicting itself on whether information should be returned, or not. This contradiction occurs within section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient is not required to return any confidential information. All copies of the Confidential Information will be retained by the Recipient for auditing purposes .
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 8 directly contradicts the original obligation to return confidential information. The original text states the Recipient 'shall promptly return all copies', whereas the changed text states the Recipient 'is not required to return any confidential information'. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document as to whether the Recipient is obligated to return confidential information upon request. The contradiction is explicitly within section 8, as stated in the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be o f any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, but it is superseded by any verbal agreements made between Sanmina and Recipient. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party, except when a Sanmina representative authorizes a change to the agreement in verbal or written form. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The original "Entire Agreement" clause states that the written document is the complete and exclusive agreement, and no modifications are valid unless in writing. The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that verbal agreements can supersede the written agreement when approved by a Sanmina Representative, therefore invalidating the claim that verbal agreements are not valid within the original written agreement. This creates uncertainty about whether the written document represents the complete agreement, because modifications can be enforced verbally as well. The contradiction is created in section 12 due to the modified clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, but it is superseded by any verbal agreements made between Sanmina and Recipient. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party, except when a Sanmina representative authorizes a change to the agreement in verbal or written form. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the entire agreement is contained within the written document and any modifications must be in writing. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that verbal agreements authorized by a Sanmina representative can supersede the written agreement. This creates a clear contradiction within section 12 itself, the 'Entire Agreement' clause, because the clause now asserts both that the written agreement is complete and exclusive, and that it can be altered by verbal agreements, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Purpose of Disclosure. The purpose of this Agreement is to permit the parties to evaluate whether to enter into a business relationship and related projects (the “Purpose”). In connection with such discussions, Sanmina may disclos e Confidential Informa - tion (defined below) to Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Purpose of Disclosure. The purpose of this Agreement is to permit the parties to evaluate whether to enter into a business relationship and related projects (the “Purpose”). In connection with such discussions, Sanmina will be required to disclose confidential information to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause in Section 1 states that Sanmina *may* disclose Confidential Information. The altered text changes this to Sanmina *will be required* to disclose Confidential Information. This changes a discretionary action into a mandatory action, which creates a contradiction because now it is uncertain, based on this section, whether disclosure of confidential information is discretionary, or required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose of Disclosure. The purpose of this Agreement is to permit the parties to evaluate whether to enter into a business relationship and related projects (the “Purpose”). In connection with such discussions, Sanmina will be required to disclose confidential information to Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Sanmina 'may' disclose confidential information, implying a discretionary action. The changed text states that Sanmina 'will be required' to disclose confidential information, making it a mandatory action. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, implying that the contradiction is within the section. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency as it is now uncertain whether the disclosure of confidential information is discretionary or required. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition as the clause is placed in a location with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the foll owing categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under th is Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (c) information which the Recipient can show was in the Recipient’s possession before the date of disclosure under this Agreement; (d) information that the Recipient can show was acquired by the Recipient from a third party who was not known by the Recipient to be under an obligation of confidence to Sanmina; and (e) information which the Recipient can show was independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Sanmina’s Confidenti al Information .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the following categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under this Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (c) information which the Recipient can show was in the Recipient’s possession before the date of disclosure under this Agreement; (d) information that the Recipient can show was acquired by the Recipient from a third party who was not known by the Recipient to be under an obligation of confidence to Sanmina; and (e) information which the Recipient can show was independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Sanmina’s Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the preceding, all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 defines categories of information not considered confidential. The added sentence "Notwithstanding the preceding, all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information." creates a direct conflict. Previously, if pricing information fell under categories (a)-(e), it wouldn't be confidential. Now, it is always confidential, no matter the condition. The contradicting aspect is created in section 3 due to the addition of the sentence, which contradicts all previous clauses within that section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Information That Is Not Confidential. Information in the following categories shall not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement: (a) information which is in the public domain at the time of the receipt under this Agreement; (b) information which comes into the public domain after receipt under this Agreement without a breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (c) information which the Recipient can show was in the Recipient’s possession before the date of disclosure under this Agreement; (d) information that the Recipient can show was acquired by the Recipient from a third party who was not known by the Recipient to be under an obligation of confidence to Sanmina; and (e) information which the Recipient can show was independently developed by the Recipient without reference to Sanmina’s Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the preceding, all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that all pricing information shall always be considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original clauses (a)-(e) of Section 3, which outline conditions under which information is *not* considered confidential. Even if pricing information fell under any of those exceptions (e.g., it was in the public domain), the added sentence now makes it automatically confidential, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and the changed text are the same, however the added clause causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law princip les. The parties agree that the state courts of Santa Clara County, California and the federal courts located in the Northern District of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement. The parties consent to the exercise by such courts of personal jurisdiction over them and each party waives any objection it might otherwise have to venue, personal jurisdiction, inconvenience of forum, and any similar or related doctrine.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that any disputes arising out of this agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City at the sole discretion of Sanmina. The parties consent to the exercise by such courts of personal jurisdiction over them and each party waives any objection it might otherwise have to venue, personal jurisdiction, inconvenience of forum, and any similar or related doctrine.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 9 states that disputes will be resolved in California courts. The changed text allows Sanmina to choose binding arbitration in New York City, directly contradicting the clause that establishes California courts as the exclusive jurisdiction. Thus, it is contradictory and uncertain as to how disputes will be resolved because it gives an option for an alternative jurisdiction. This contradiction is present in section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties agree that any disputes arising out of this agreement may be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City at the sole discretion of Sanmina. The parties consent to the exercise by such courts of personal jurisdiction over them and each party waives any objection it might otherwise have to venue, personal jurisdiction, inconvenience of forum, and any similar or related doctrine.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be resolved in California courts. The changed text introduces the possibility of binding arbitration in New York City at Sanmina's discretion. This directly contradicts the clause establishing California courts as the exclusive jurisdiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as the contradiction exists within the amended section 9.
